faculty.name: Vision:

qualifications.degree: research and consulting practices. The Department will cultivate values of honesty and transparency
commitment to quality and high standards

passion for performance and sensitivity to social and ethical issues. To work in collaboration with industries and research establishments in the design and development process to understand the industry practices and diffuse through training. Programmes: The Department of Computer Science and Engineering
International Institute of Information Technology Bhubaneswar
and at present offers the following four years undergraduate programmes

two years Master Degree programmes and Doctoral Degree Programme as follows : B.Tech. degree in Computer Science & Engineering Three Branches are coming under the B.Tech. Program: CSE (Computer Science & Engineering) IT (Information Technology) CE (Computer Engineering) M.Tech degree in Computer Science & Engineering Ph.D. degree in Computer Science & Engineering It has produced high quality technocrats for the last few decades to cater to the needs of hardware and software industry

and academic Institutions. The department has been accredited for 3 years since 2006 by AICTE with the extension of U.G.C. conferred academic autonomy. The syllabi of the Department are updated with many advanced courses to enable students to keep themselves at par with the cutting edge-technology. The teaching in the department emphasises on fundamental principles
development of creative thinking and the analytical ability to solve real life problems. The Department also encourages its students to engage in extra-curricular and co-curricular activities

and developing organisational skills. The Faculty members of the department are involved in diversified advanced research areas like: Data Communication and Networking Data Mining & Soft Computing Distributed Systems Soft Computing Image and Video Processing Internet of Things Information Security Cloud Computing Blockchain Bioinformatics 5G and Advance Wireless Technology Most of the faculty members have received advanced degrees and/or training from reputed institutions in India. Alumni members of the department hold prestigious positions in leading Software and Hardware companies like Microsoft

CTS etc in various countries all over the world. Many alumni have been employed in reputed public sector undertakings and academic institutions in India. The department has adequate infrastructures including new laboratories namely Windows NT

PC laboratories and one research laboratory for PhD and M.Tech. scholars. The faculty members and students of the department have been honoured at National and International level. The department organises National Technical Seminars
Industry-Institute Interaction Programme every year. Dr. Ajaya kumar Dash Computer Science and Engineering (HOD) Message from the Director As we begin a new academic year
I would like to warmly greet all the members of our Computer Science Department. Our field is at the front of innovation
always progressing and shaping the future. It is an exciting time to be a part of this dynamic discipline. We

are committed to provide our students with the expertise and understanding required to thrive in the current highly competitive employment landscape. The department takes great pride in cultivating a nurturing and all-encompassing atmosphere. We strongly advocate for diversity and are committed to fostering an environment that promotes inclusivity and enables everyone to reach their maximum capabilities. Your success is our triumph and we are dedicated to assisting you at every stage of your journey. Research continues to be a fundamental and essential aspect of our department. I encourage all students and teachers to actively participate in research endeavours and take advantage of the resources and possibilities available. I appreciate your ongoing commitment
and look forward to witnessing the incredible achievements that we will accomplish together in the coming year. Dr. Ajaya Kumar Dash Department Faculties Dr. Ajaya Kumar Dash HOD

Development” About: Capacity building in the area of Information Security to address the human resource requirement of the country. Generation of core research manpower to undertake basic/fundamental research

research in the area of Product/Solution design and development and in selected thematic areas of national strategic importance to build indigenous capability. Introduction of Information Security curriculum in formal courses like M.Tech./M.E./M.S.
modular/short term knowledge oriented courses etc. through academic institutions
Launching non-formal modular/short-term knowledge-cum-skill oriented courses
for working professionals at all levels including the flexible certificate programs

etc. Launching formal courses on virtual mode using the NKN Network to expand the capacities. Training of Government personnel. Creation of mass information security awareness targeted towards: Academic Users: School level – Children
Cyber cafes and general public at large. Government Users: Central/State Government employees (non IT professionals)
etc. Creating a national repository of courses designed course contents and learning material
MIS about the professionals trained through this scheme
changes in the market trends and the resultant human resource requirement etc. Funding agency Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology

Government of India Funding Amount 63 lakhs Duration Period of five years starting from 16th November 2015 Chief-Investigator Dr Debasish Jena Co-Chief-Investigator Dr Suvendu Rup DST-FIST “Bioinformatics Lab” About: DST

Govt. of India has identified Department of CSE for strengthening the existing infrastructure in Level1 category for setting up a Bioinformatics Research Lab under “Fund for Improvement of S&T infrastructure in universities & higher educational institutions (FIST)-2013” program. Prof. Anjali Mohapatra is the Program-Coordinator of this lab. This Lab provides the computational facilities to the under graduate students
research scholars and faculty members across the departments. It is equipped with high-end workstations

3D viewing screen including advanced software tools for simulation and modeling of Bioinformatics- Applications as well as development and analysis of the Algorithms related to this area. Funding agency DST-FIST Funding Amount 50 Lakhs Duration Period of five years starting from 2014 Chief-Investigator Co-Chief-Investigator Cross Lingual Information Access – II About: Cross Lingual Information Access-II is funded by Department of Information Technology
Ministry of Communication and Information Technology
Government of India as a part of CLIA consortium. The CLIA consortium consists of 12 institutes (AU-KBC Chennai

Gauhati University) with IIT Bombay as leader. The objective of the project is to design an online search engine for Indian languages like Google. Indian language search system (Sandhan) has been developed by the CLIA consortium. Funding agency Department of Electronics and Information Technology

Government of India Funding Amount 70.84 Lakhs Duration Period of five years starting from 2012 Chief-Investigator Dr Rakesh Chandra Balabantaray Co-Chief-Investigator Secure Digitization Land Record using Blockchain Technology About: Secure Digitization of Land Record using Blockchain Technology is funded by Department of Technology

Government of Odisha under Research and Development project. The duration of the project is three years. The objective of the project is to develop a system using blockchain technology for digital land record of odisha state which will be transparent and secure. The fraud in land selling and purchase could be reduced. Funding agency Department of Science and Technology

Government of Odisha Funding Amount 9.90 Lakhs Duration Period of three years starting from 06th March 2020 Chief-Investigator Dr. Debasish Jena Co-Chief-Investigator Dr. Suraj Sharma Intelligent Interface About: Intelligent interface
to enhance efficient interactions between user and databases
is the need of the database applications. Databases must be intelligent enough to make the accessibility faster. However
not every user familiar with the Structured Query Language (SQL) queries as they may not aware of structure of the database and they thus require to learn SQL. So
non-expert users need a system to interact with relational databases in their natural language such as English. For this
Database Management System (DBMS) must have an ability to understand Natural Language (NL). In this research

an intelligent interface shall be developed which translates natural language query to SQL using machine learning algorithms. Funding agency Honeywell Funding Amount 1080000 INR Duration 3 Years Chief-Investigator Dr. Rakesh Chandra Balabantaray Co-Chief-Investigator Indian Language to Indian Language Machine Translation About: English-IL and IL-IL machine translation plays an important role in the larger Speech to Speech Machine Translation in the Indian context. The proposed project aims to develop Machine Translation systems. It is a consortium mode project with 11 Institutes headed by IIIT Hyderabad. Funding agency Consortium mode project with 11 Institutes headed by IIIT Hyderabad Funding Amount 2524.12 Lakh INR Duration 3 Years Chief-Investigator Dr. Rakesh Chandra Balabantaray Co-Chief-Investigator English to Indian Language & Vice Versa Machine Translation system About: Language technology has reached an advanced level of maturity today

where it is making mass impact on users of English and many Indian languages of the world on various aspects of the language use. Indian language technology can enable people to access information in their own language. NLP technologies are the key to connect billions of people around the globe to access and avail the facilities

and benefits of the internet from education to communication and many other applications that can be provided to the future generations in their language of conveniences. This project proposes a Machine Translation System to facilitate translation (text-to-text) from English to Hindi
Malayalam and Dogri languages and vice-versa covering various domains (Governance and Policy (Primary)

Agriculture). It is a consortium mode project with 10 Institutes headed by CDAC Pune.
